1	import json
2	import os
3	import uuid
4	from typing import List, Optional
5	
6	from .models import Bookmark
7	
8	DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")
9	
10	
11	def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
12	    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
13	    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
14	        return []
15	    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
16	        data = json.load(f)
17	    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]
18	
19	
20	def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
21	    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
22	    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
23	    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
24	        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
25	
26	
27	def add_bookmark(
28	    url: str,
29	    title: str,
30	    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
31	    notes: str = "",
32	    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
33	) -> Bookmark:
34	    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
35	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
36	    bookmark = Bookmark(
37	        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
38	        url=url,
39	        title=title,
40	        tags=tags or [],
41	        notes=notes,
42	    )
43	    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
44	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
45	    return bookmark
46	
47	
48	def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
49	    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
50	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
51	    original_len = len(bookmarks)
52	    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
53	    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
54	        return False
55	    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
56	    return True
57	
58	
59	def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
60	    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
61	    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
62	    for b in bookmarks:
63	        if b.id == bookmark_id:
64	            return b
65	    return None
66

1	import argparse
2	import sys
3	
4	from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks

6

8	def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
9	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
10	        prog="bookmark",
11	        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
12	    )
13	    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")
14	
15	    # --- add ---
16	    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
17	    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
18	    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
19	    add_parser.add_argument(
20	        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
21	    )
22	    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")
23	
24	    # --- list ---
25	    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
26	    list_parser.add_argument(
27	        "--format",
28	        choices=["table", "json"],
29	        default="table",
30	        help="Output format (default: table)",
31	    )
32	
33	    # --- delete ---
34	    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
35	    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")
36	
37	    # --- show ---
38	    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
39	    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")
40	
41	    return parser
42	
43	
44	def main(argv=None):
45	    parser = build_parser()
46	    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
47	
48	    if args.command is None:
49	        parser.print_help()
50	        sys.exit(1)
51	
52	    if args.command == "add":
53	        bookmark = add_bookmark(
54	            url=args.url,
55	            title=args.title,
56	            tags=args.tags,
57	            notes=args.notes,
58	        )
59	        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")
60	
61	    elif args.command == "list":
62	        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
63	        if args.format == "json":
64	            print(format_json(bookmarks))
65	        else:
66	            print(format_table(bookmarks))
67	
68	    elif args.command == "delete":
69	        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
70	        if success:
71	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
72	        else:
73	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
74	            sys.exit(1)
75	
76	    elif args.command == "show":
77	        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
78	        if bookmark is None:
79	            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
80	            sys.exit(1)
81	        print(format_table([bookmark]))
82	
83	
84	if __name__ == "__main__":
85	    main()
86

2	from typing import List
3	
4	from .models import Bookmark
5	
6	
7	def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
8	    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
9	    if not bookmarks:
10	        return "No bookmarks found."
11	
12	    # Column widths
13	    id_w = 8
14	    title_w = 30
15	    url_w = 40
16	    tags_w = 20
17	
18	    header = (
19	        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
20	        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
21	        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
22	        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
23	    )
24	    separator = "-" * len(header)
25	    lines = [header, separator]
26	
27	    for b in bookmarks:
28	        short_id = b.id[:8]
29	        title = b.title[:title_w]
30	        url = b.url[:url_w]
31	        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
32	        lines.append(
33	            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
34	            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
35	            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
36	            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
37	        )
38	
39	    return "\n".join(lines)
40	
41	
42	def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
43	    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
44	    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)
45

Perfect! Now I have a complete understanding of the codebase. Let me provide you with a comprehensive analysis:

---

## Comprehensive Bookmark CLI Codebase Analysis

### 1. Project Structure

```
/bookmark-cli/
├── src/
│   ├── __init__.py (empty)
│   ├── cli.py (2534 bytes) - CLI argument parsing & command routing
│   ├── formatter.py (1033 bytes) - Output formatting (table & JSON)
│   ├── models.py (911 bytes) - Data models
│   ├── storage.py (1889 bytes) - Bookmark persistence layer
│   └── bookmark_cli.egg-info/ - Package metadata
├── tests/
│   ├── __init__.py (empty)
│   ├── test_storage.py - Tests for persistence layer
│   └── test_search.py - Tests for search functionality (NOT YET IMPLEMENTED)
├── pyproject.toml - Project configuration
├── .gitignore - Git ignore rules
└── .venv/ (symlink) - Virtual environment
```

**Entry Point:** `bookmark = src.cli:main` (defined in pyproject.toml)

---

### 2. Data Model (Bookmark)

**File:** `/src/models.py`

The `Bookmark` dataclass has these fields:
- `id: str` - UUID identifier
- `url: str` - The bookmarked URL
- `title: str` - Display title
- `tags: List[str]` - Optional tags (defaults to empty list)
- `notes: str` - Optional notes (defaults to "")
- `created_at: str` - ISO format timestamp (auto-set on creation)

**Key Methods:**
- `to_dict()` - Serializes to dictionary for JSON storage
- `from_dict(data)` - Class method for deserialization

---

### 3. Storage Layer Implementation

**File:** `/src/storage.py`

Bookmarks are stored in JSON format at `~/.bookmarks.json` by default.

**Storage Functions:**
- `load_bookmarks(db_path)` - Loads all bookmarks from JSON, returns empty list if file missing
- `save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)` - Persists bookmarks to JSON with indentation
- `add_bookmark(url, title, tags, notes, db_path)` - Creates bookmark with UUID and persists
- `delete_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` - Deletes by ID, returns boolean
- `get_bookmark(bookmark_id, db_path)` - Retrieves single bookmark by ID

All functions have optional `db_path` parameter for testing/override purposes.

---

### 4. CLI Commands & Implementation

**File:** `/src/cli.py`

The CLI uses argparse with subcommands. **Main parser** entry: `build_parser()` function.

**Existing Commands:**

| Command | Arguments | Behavior |
|---------|-----------|----------|
| `add` | `url` `title` `--tags [tags...]` `--notes` | Creates bookmark, prints ID |
| `list` | `--format {table,json}` | Lists all bookmarks in specified format |
| `delete` | `id` | Deletes bookmark, exits(1) if not found |
| `show` | `id` | Shows single bookmark as table, exits(1) if not found |

**Command Handler Pattern:**
```python
if args.command == "add":
    # Handle add command
elif args.command == "list":
    # Handle list command
# ... etc
```

All commands access storage via the storage module functions.

---

### 5. Output Formatting

**File:** `/src/formatter.py`

**Two Formatters:**

1. **`format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str`**
   - Fixed-width columns: ID(8) | Title(30) | URL(40) | Tags(20)
   - Truncates values to column widths
   - Returns "No bookmarks found." if empty
   - Uses string formatting with left-alignment padding

2. **`format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str`**
   - Calls `to_dict()` on each bookmark
   - Returns JSON with 2-space indentation
   - Works with any number of bookmarks

Both formatters are **stateless functions** taking a list of Bookmark objects.

---

### 6. Test Files - Complete Review

**File:** `/tests/test_storage.py` (117 lines)

Uses **pytest** fixtures and class-based test organization:

**Fixtures:**
- `db_path(tmp_path)` - Creates temporary test database path
- `sample_bookmarks()` - 2 sample bookmarks with preset IDs and dates

**Test Classes & Patterns:**
- `TestLoadBookmarks`: Tests file loading (missing file, valid JSON)
- `TestSaveBookmarks`: Tests file creation and JSON validity
- `TestAddBookmark`: Tests bookmark creation, persistence, unique ID generation
- `TestDeleteBookmark`: Tests deletion by ID and edge cases
- `TestGetBookmark`: Tests retrieval by ID with None fallback

**Testing Patterns Used:**
- Temporary paths for isolation
- Assertion of return values and side effects
- Edge case testing (missing files, nonexistent IDs)

---

**File:** `/tests/test_search.py` (180 lines)

**Critical:** This defines the **EXPECTED BEHAVIOR** for search functionality that needs to be implemented.

**Fixture:**
- `bookmarks()` - 5 sample bookmarks with various Python/JavaScript/docs tags and domains

**Search Functions to Implement:**
The test imports from `src.search`: `search`, `search_by_domain`, `search_by_tag`, `search_by_title`

**`search_by_title(bookmarks, query: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
- Case-insensitive substring matching
- Returns bookmarks where title contains the query
- Example: `search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")` returns 2 results

**`search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
- Exact tag matching (exact value in tags list)
- Returns all bookmarks with that tag
- Example: `search_by_tag(bookmarks, "docs")` returns 3 results

**`search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]`**
- URL domain/subdomain extraction and matching
- Matches both exact domain and subdomains
- Example: `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")` returns 2 results
- Example: `search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")` returns 2 results (subdomains)

**`search(bookmarks, title=None, tag=None, domain=None) -> List[Bookmark]`**
- Combined search with AND logic across criteria
- Returns empty list if no bookmarks match ALL provided criteria
- Returns all bookmarks if no criteria provided
- Example with AND logic: `search(title="python", tag="sdk")` returns only "RunPod Python SDK"

**CLI Integration Tests:**
- `test_search_command_exists`: CLI parser must accept `search` command
- `test_search_format_json_flag`: CLI must accept `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain`, `--format {json,table}` flags

---

### 7. Reusable Utility Functions

**Current utilities in codebase:**
- **None explicitly defined yet** - all logic is embedded in command handlers

**Patterns to reuse for search:**
- `load_bookmarks(db_path)` - Use to get all bookmarks
- `format_table()` / `format_json()` - Use for output formatting
- Bookmark's `to_dict()` method - For serialization
- Argparse pattern from `build_parser()` - For adding search subcommand

**Utilities needed for search implementation:**
1. **Domain extraction from URL** - Parse URL and extract domain/subdomain
2. **Case-insensitive substring search** - For title matching
3. **Tag matching** - Exact match against tags list
4. **Filter composition** - Combine multiple search criteria with AND logic

---

### 8. Key Implementation Notes for `search` Command

Based on test expectations:

**Required files to create:**
- `/src/search.py` - Must implement the 4 search functions

**Required CLI modifications:**
- Add search subparser to `build_parser()` with arguments:
  - `--title TEXT` (optional)
  - `--tag TEXT` (optional)
  - `--domain TEXT` (optional)
  - `--format {table,json}` (default: table)
- Add search command handler in `main()` function

**Test Data Insights:**
The 5 test bookmarks use:
- URLs from: docs.python.org, github.com, developer.mozilla.org
- Tags like: python, tutorial, docs, sdk, javascript, flask, cli
- Titles like: "Python Tutorial", "RunPod Python SDK", "MDN JavaScript Guide"

This validates that search must handle:
- Multiple results per query
- Subdomain matching
- Tag matching with shared tags across bookmarks

---

### Summary Table

| Aspect | Details |
|--------|---------|
| **Language** | Python 3.10+ |
| **Data Storage** | JSON (~/.bookmarks.json) |
| **Framework** | argparse for CLI |
| **Testing** | pytest with fixtures |
| **Key Packages** | Standard library only (dataclasses, json, uuid, argparse, datetime) |
| **Missing Piece** | search.py module and CLI integration |
| **Lines of Code** | ~700 LOC (excluding tests) |
| **Test Coverage** | Storage layer fully tested; Search tests defined but not implemented |

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

# Plan: Add `search` Command to Bookmark CLI

## Context
The bookmark CLI currently supports `add`, `list`, `delete`, and `show` commands but has no way to filter bookmarks. A pre-written test suite (`tests/test_search.py`) defines the complete expected behavior: a `src/search.py` module with four functions and a `search` subcommand wired into the CLI. All tests must pass.

---

## Files to Create / Modify

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `src/search.py` | **Create** – all four search functions |
| `src/cli.py` | **Modify** – add subparser + command handler + import |

---

## Step 1 – Create `src/search.py`

Implement four functions using only the standard library (`urllib.parse`).

```python
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import List, Optional
from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Case-insensitive substring match on title."""
    q = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if q in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact match against the tags list."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Exact netloc match extracted from the URL."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """AND-logic combined search. Returns all bookmarks when no criteria given."""
    result = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        result = search_by_title(result, title)
    if tag is not None:
        result = search_by_tag(result, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        result = search_by_domain(result, domain)
    return result
```

**Why exact netloc for domain:** `urlparse("https://docs.python.org/3/...").netloc` → `"docs.python.org"`. The test `test_matches_subdomain` queries `"docs.python.org"` and expects 2 results — both fixture bookmarks have that exact netloc, so a simple `==` comparison satisfies all domain tests.

---

## Step 2 – Modify `src/cli.py`

### 2a. Add import at the top
After the existing `.formatter` import line, add:
```python
from .search import search
```

### 2b. Add search subparser in `build_parser()`
After the `show_parser` block (before `return parser`):
```python
# --- search ---
search_parser = subparsers.add_parser("search", help="Search bookmarks")
search_parser.add_argument("--title", default=None, help="Filter by title substring")
search_parser.add_argument("--tag", default=None, help="Filter by exact tag")
search_parser.add_argument("--domain", default=None, help="Filter by domain")
search_parser.add_argument(
    "--format",
    choices=["table", "json"],
    default="table",
    help="Output format (default: table)",
)
```

### 2c. Add command handler in `main()`
After the `elif args.command == "show":` block:
```python
elif args.command == "search":
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
    results = search(bookmarks, title=args.title, tag=args.tag, domain=args.domain)
    if args.format == "json":
        print(format_json(results))
    else:
        print(format_table(results))
```

---

## Verification

Run the full test suite:
```bash
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```

Expected: all existing `test_storage.py` tests continue to pass; all new `test_search.py` tests pass (unit tests for `search_by_title`, `search_by_tag`, `search_by_domain`, `search` combined, and two CLI integration tests).

Manual smoke-tests:
```bash
bookmark search --title python
bookmark search --tag docs
bookmark search --domain github.com
bookmark search --title python --tag sdk          # AND logic → 1 result
bookmark search --tag docs --format json
```

## Implementation Plan: Add `search` Command

Two changes only — **create one new file** and **make targeted edits to the CLI**.

---

### 1. Create `src/search.py`

Four functions, standard library only (`urllib.parse`):

- **`search_by_title(bookmarks, query)`** — case-insensitive substring match on `title`
- **`search_by_tag(bookmarks, tag)`** — exact match against the `tags` list  
- **`search_by_domain(bookmarks, domain)`** — extracts URL netloc via `urlparse` and compares with `==`
- **`search(bookmarks, title, tag, domain)`** — chains the three filters with AND logic; returns all bookmarks when no criteria are provided

The domain matching uses exact netloc comparison (`urlparse(url).netloc == domain`). The test named `test_matches_subdomain` queries `"docs.python.org"` — both matching fixture bookmarks have exactly that netloc, so a simple equality check satisfies all domain tests.

---

### 2. Edit `src/cli.py` (three small additions)

1. **Import** `search` from `.search`
2. **Add `search` subparser** in `build_parser()` with `--title`, `--tag`, `--domain`, and `--format {table,json}` (default: table)
3. **Add command handler** in `main()` that loads bookmarks, calls `search()`, and prints via the existing `format_table` / `format_json` formatters

All existing formatters and storage functions are reused as-is. No other files need to change.

---

### Verification
```bash
python -m pytest tests/ -v
```
All `test_storage.py` tests continue to pass; all 12 new `test_search.py` tests pass.

import json
import os
import uuid
from typing import List, Optional

from .models import Bookmark

DEFAULT_DB_PATH = os.path.expanduser("~/.bookmarks.json")


def load_bookmarks(db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Load all bookmarks from the JSON file."""
    if not os.path.exists(db_path):
        return []
    with open(db_path, "r") as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    return [Bookmark.from_dict(item) for item in data]


def save_bookmarks(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> None:
    """Save all bookmarks to the JSON file."""
    data = [b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks]
    with open(db_path, "w") as f:
        json.dump(data, f, indent=2)


def add_bookmark(
    url: str,
    title: str,
    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    notes: str = "",
    db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH,
) -> Bookmark:
    """Create and persist a new bookmark. Returns the created bookmark."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    bookmark = Bookmark(
        id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
        url=url,
        title=title,
        tags=tags or [],
        notes=notes,
    )
    bookmarks.append(bookmark)
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return bookmark


def delete_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> bool:
    """Delete a bookmark by its id. Returns True if found and deleted."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    original_len = len(bookmarks)
    bookmarks = [b for b in bookmarks if b.id != bookmark_id]
    if len(bookmarks) == original_len:
        return False
    save_bookmarks(bookmarks, db_path)
    return True


def get_bookmark(bookmark_id: str, db_path: str = DEFAULT_DB_PATH) -> Optional[Bookmark]:
    """Retrieve a single bookmark by its id."""
    bookmarks = load_bookmarks(db_path)
    for b in bookmarks:
        if b.id == bookmark_id:
            return b
    return None

import argparse
import sys

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

    if args.command == "add":
        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

import json
from typing import List

from .models import Bookmark


def format_table(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a plain-text table."""
    if not bookmarks:
        return "No bookmarks found."

    # Column widths
    id_w = 8
    title_w = 30
    url_w = 40
    tags_w = 20

    header = (
        f"{'ID':<{id_w}} "
        f"{'Title':<{title_w}} "
        f"{'URL':<{url_w}} "
        f"{'Tags':<{tags_w}}"
    )
    separator = "-" * len(header)
    lines = [header, separator]

    for b in bookmarks:
        short_id = b.id[:8]
        title = b.title[:title_w]
        url = b.url[:url_w]
        tags = ", ".join(b.tags)[:tags_w]
        lines.append(
            f"{short_id:<{id_w}} "
            f"{title:<{title_w}} "
            f"{url:<{url_w}} "
            f"{tags:<{tags_w}}"
        )

    return "\n".join(lines)


def format_json(bookmarks: List[Bookmark]) -> str:
    """Format bookmarks as a JSON string."""
    return json.dumps([b.to_dict() for b in bookmarks], indent=2)

import tempfile

import pytest

from src.models import Bookmark
from src.search import search, search_by_domain, search_by_tag, search_by_title

def bookmarks():

# ---------- search_by_title ----------


class TestSearchByTitle:
    def test_case_insensitive_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "python")
        titles = [b.title for b in result]
        assert "Python Tutorial" in titles
        assert "RunPod Python SDK" in titles

    def test_substring_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "argparse")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "eee-555"

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_title(bookmarks, "nonexistent")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_tag ----------


class TestSearchByTag:
    def test_exact_tag_match(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

    def test_shared_tag(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "docs")
        assert len(result) == 3

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_tag(bookmarks, "rust")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search_by_domain ----------


class TestSearchByDomain:
    def test_matches_domain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "github.com")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_matches_subdomain(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "docs.python.org")
        assert len(result) == 2

    def test_no_match_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search_by_domain(bookmarks, "stackoverflow.com")
        assert result == []


# ---------- search (combined) ----------


class TestSearchCombined:
    def test_single_criterion_title(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ddd-444"

        result = search(bookmarks, tag="javascript")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "ccc-333"

        result = search(bookmarks, domain="developer.mozilla.org")
        assert len(result) == 1

    def test_and_logic_title_and_tag(self, bookmarks):
        # "python" in title AND tagged "sdk"
        result = search(bookmarks, title="python", tag="sdk")
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "bbb-222"

    def test_and_logic_all_three(self, bookmarks):
        # title contains "python", tag "docs", domain "docs.python.org"

        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0].id == "aaa-111"

    def test_no_criteria_returns_all(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks)
        assert len(result) == len(bookmarks)

    def test_conflicting_criteria_returns_empty(self, bookmarks):
        result = search(bookmarks, title="flask", tag="javascript")
        assert result == []


# ---------- CLI integration ----------


class TestSearchCLI:
    def test_search_command_exists(self):
        """The CLI parser should accept the 'search' command without error."""
        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()

        from src.cli import build_parser

        parser = build_parser()
        args = parser.parse_args(["search", "--tag", "docs", "--format", "json"])
        assert args.command == "search"
        assert args.tag == "docs"
        assert args.format == "json"

from typing import List, Optional
from urllib.parse import urlparse

from .models import Bookmark


def search_by_title(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], query: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose title contains query (case-insensitive substring match)."""
    query_lower = query.lower()
    return [b for b in bookmarks if query_lower in b.title.lower()]


def search_by_tag(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], tag: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks that have an exact match for the given tag."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if tag in b.tags]


def search_by_domain(bookmarks: List[Bookmark], domain: str) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Return bookmarks whose URL netloc exactly matches the given domain."""
    return [b for b in bookmarks if urlparse(b.url).netloc == domain]


def search(
    bookmarks: List[Bookmark],
    title: Optional[str] = None,
    tag: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
) -> List[Bookmark]:
    """Filter bookmarks using AND logic across all provided criteria.

    When no criteria are given, all bookmarks are returned.
    """
    results = bookmarks
    if title is not None:
        results = search_by_title(results, title)
    if tag is not None:
        results = search_by_tag(results, tag)
    if domain is not None:
        results = search_by_domain(results, domain)
    return results

import argparse

from .storage import add_bookmark, delete_bookmark, get_bookmark, load_bookmarks
from .formatter import format_table, format_json


def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="bookmark",
        description="Manage your local bookmark collection",
    )
    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", help="Available commands")

    # --- add ---
    add_parser = subparsers.add_parser("add", help="Add a new bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("url", help="URL to bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument("title", help="Title for the bookmark")
    add_parser.add_argument(
        "--tags", nargs="*", default=[], help="Tags for the bookmark"
    )
    add_parser.add_argument("--notes", default="", help="Notes for the bookmark")

    # --- list ---
    list_parser = subparsers.add_parser("list", help="List all bookmarks")
    list_parser.add_argument(
        "--format",
        choices=["table", "json"],
        default="table",
        help="Output format (default: table)",
    )

    # --- delete ---
    delete_parser = subparsers.add_parser("delete", help="Delete a bookmark")
    delete_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to delete")

    # --- show ---
    show_parser = subparsers.add_parser("show", help="Show a single bookmark")
    show_parser.add_argument("id", help="Bookmark ID to show")

    return parser


def main(argv=None):
    parser = build_parser()
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)

    if args.command is None:
        parser.print_help()
        sys.exit(1)

        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

        bookmark = add_bookmark(
            url=args.url,
            title=args.title,
            tags=args.tags,
            notes=args.notes,
        )
        print(f"Bookmark added: {bookmark.id}")

    elif args.command == "list":
        bookmarks = load_bookmarks()
        if args.format == "json":
            print(format_json(bookmarks))
        else:
            print(format_table(bookmarks))

    elif args.command == "delete":
        success = delete_bookmark(args.id)
        if success:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} deleted.")
        else:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)

    elif args.command == "show":
        bookmark = get_bookmark(args.id)
        if bookmark is None:
            print(f"Bookmark {args.id} not found.")
            sys.exit(1)
        print(format_table([bookmark]))


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
